texts.memory_archival_failed_session: Memory archival failed, session not cleared. Please try again.
texts.memory_archival_failed_persona: Memory archival failed, persona not switched. Please try again.
texts.cmd_persona_set: /persona set <name> — Switch persona and start a new session
texts.cmd_stchar: /stchar <list|show|load> ... — Browse persona assets with NanoMate-style aliases
texts.cmd_preset: /preset [show [persona]] — Inspect imported preset assets for a persona
texts.cmd_mcp: /mcp [list] — List configured MCP servers and registered tools
texts.cmd_update: /update [check|force|bridge] — Inspect or run checkout sync, component refresh, and restart
texts.cmd_dream_log: /dream-log — Show what the last Dream changed
texts.cmd_dream_restore: /dream-restore — Revert memory to a previous state
texts.cmd_session_current: /session current — Show the active session key for this chat
texts.cmd_session_list: /session list — List recent saved sessions in the active workspace
texts.cmd_session_export: /session export [key] — Export one saved session to workspace/out/sessions/
texts.cmd_session_use: /session use <key> — Route this chat to another saved session
texts.cmd_session_new: /session new [name] — Create and switch this chat to a fresh session key
texts.cmd_repo: /repo <status|diff> ... — Inspect the active workspace Git state without mutating it
texts.cmd_review: /review [staged|base <rev>|path <repo-path>] — Review the local Git diff with the configured model
texts.cmd_compact: /compact [key] — Manually run session token compaction for the active or named session
texts.skill_usage:
Usage:
/skill search <query>
/skill install <slug>
/skill uninstall <slug>
/skill list
/skill update
/skill derive <name> [brief] [--force]
/skill supersede <newer> <older> [more...]
/skill supersede remove <newer> <older> [more...]
/skill supersede clear <newer>
/skill lint
texts.skill_search_no_results: No skills found for "{query}". Try broader keywords, or use /skill install <slug> if you know the exact slug.
texts.skill_search_timeout: ClawHub search timed out. Check network, proxy, or registry connectivity and retry.
texts.skill_search_request_failed: ClawHub search request failed. Check network, proxy, or registry connectivity and retry.
texts.skill_search_invalid_response: ClawHub search returned an unexpected response.
texts.skill_supersede_missing_args:
Missing workspace skill or superseded target.

Usage:
/skill supersede <newer> <older> [more...]
/skill supersede remove <newer> <older> [more...]
/skill supersede clear <newer>
texts.skill_derive_exists: A workspace skill draft for {slug} already exists at {path}. Re-run with /skill derive {slug} --force to overwrite it.
texts.skill_derive_failed: Failed to write the workspace skill draft {slug} at {path}.
texts.skill_derive_review_hint: Review and tighten the draft before relying on it in future runs.
texts.skill_supersede_not_workspace: Skill {slug} is not a workspace skill editable at {path}. Only workspace skills can update supersedes metadata.
texts.skill_supersede_failed: Failed to update supersedes metadata for {slug} at {path}.
texts.skill_supersede_removed:
Updated workspace skill {slug} at {path}.
Remaining supersedes: {current}
texts.skill_supersede_remove_unchanged:
Workspace skill {slug} does not currently supersede any of: {targets}
Current supersedes: {current}
texts.skill_supersede_cleared: Cleared supersedes metadata for workspace skill {slug} at {path}.
texts.skill_supersede_already_clear: Workspace skill {slug} already has no supersedes metadata.
texts.skill_lint_header: Skill lint summary: {total} total, {visible} visible in runtime summary.
texts.skill_lint_clean: No obvious supersedes or overlap problems detected.
texts.skill_lint_superseded_header: Superseded skills hidden from runtime summary:
texts.skill_uninstall_failed: Failed to remove local skill {slug} at {path}.
texts.skill_lockfile_cleanup_failed: Removed the local skill, but could not update the ClawHub lockfile at {path}.
texts.skill_npx_missing: npx is not installed. Install Node.js first, then retry /skill.
texts.skill_command_timeout: The ClawHub command timed out. Check npm connectivity or proxy settings and try again.
texts.skill_command_failed: ClawHub command failed with exit code {code}.
texts.skill_command_network_failed: ClawHub could not reach the npm registry. Check your network, proxy, or npm registry configuration and retry.
texts.mcp_no_servers: No MCP servers are configured for this agent.
texts.mcp_no_tools: No MCP tools are currently registered. Check MCP server connectivity and configuration.
texts.switched_persona: Switched persona to {persona}. New session started.
texts.stchar_missing_name:
Missing persona name.

Usage:
/stchar show <name>
/stchar load <name>
texts.scene_usage:
Usage:
/scene list
/scene daily
/scene comfort
/scene date
/scene <custom_scene>
/scene generate <brief>
texts.scene_tool_disabled: Image generation is not enabled. Turn on tools.imageGen.enabled first.
texts.scene_caption_daily: {persona} sent over a quiet daily snapshot.
texts.scene_caption_comfort: {persona} stayed close and left you a comforting scene.
texts.scene_caption_date: {persona} made a date photo for you.
texts.scene_caption_generate: {persona} turned your idea into a companion scene.
texts.updating_started: Starting update: syncing the Git checkout, dependencies, and enabled components. The process will restart automatically if everything succeeds.
texts.updating_force_started: Starting force update: the clean-working-tree precheck will be skipped, then the checkout, dependencies, and enabled components will be refreshed. The process will restart automatically if everything succeeds.
texts.updating_bridge_started: Starting WhatsApp bridge refresh. The process will restart automatically if the rebuild succeeds.
texts.update_error_not_checkout: This runtime is not running from a Git checkout, so /update is unavailable.
texts.update_error_git_missing: git is not available in PATH, so /update cannot continue.
texts.update_error_uv_missing: uv is not available in PATH, so /update cannot continue.
texts.update_error_npm_missing: WhatsApp is enabled, but npm is not available in PATH, so the bridge cannot be refreshed.
texts.update_error_detached_head: The current checkout is in detached HEAD state. /update requires a branch with upstream tracking.
texts.update_error_missing_upstream: The current branch does not track an upstream, so /update cannot run git pull safely.
texts.update_error_dirty_worktree:
The working tree is not clean. Commit, stash, or discard local changes before running /update.

{changes}
texts.update_error_bridge_source_missing: The bundled WhatsApp bridge source is missing at {path}.
texts.update_error_bridge_install_failed: npm install failed while refreshing the WhatsApp bridge.
texts.update_error_bridge_build_failed: npm run build failed while refreshing the WhatsApp bridge.
texts.update_step_bridge_refresh: Refresh and rebuild the local WhatsApp bridge
texts.start_greeting:
Hi {name}. I'm hahobot.

Send me a message and I'll respond.
Type /help to see available commands.
texts.admin_login_missing_key_body: Admin is enabled, but gateway.admin.authKey is empty in this instance config. Set a non-empty key in config.json first.
texts.admin_login_missing_key_error: Admin auth key is not configured.
texts.admin_overview_workspace_mismatch: The running gateway workspace no longer matches agents.defaults.workspace in config.json. Persona edits below still target the runtime workspace used when this process started. Restart the process if you want to switch workspaces.
texts.admin_overview_recent_sessions_desc: Saved chat history from the current runtime workspace. Open the dedicated Sessions page for a fuller drill-down view.
texts.admin_scope_text: current config.json and current runtime workspace only
texts.admin_card_config_desc: Edit the active instance config.json through a visual form, with raw JSON as a fallback.
texts.admin_card_sessions_desc: Browse saved sessions in the current runtime workspace, grouped by source with recent message previews.
texts.admin_card_skills_desc: Inspect built-in and workspace skills, including availability, always-on state, and runtime-hidden skills.
texts.admin_card_cron_desc: Inspect the workspace cron store and recent scheduler state without switching to raw JSON first.
texts.admin_card_weixin_desc: Start or refresh the built-in personal Weixin QR login flow without leaving the admin UI.
texts.admin_card_commands_desc: Review the chat slash commands, aliases, and concrete usage supported by this project.
texts.admin_card_personas_desc: Edit prompt files such as SOUL.md, USER.md, optional PROFILE.md / INSIGHTS.md, persona-local VOICE.json, and st_manifest.json in the current runtime workspace.
texts.admin_sessions_intro: This page reads saved session files from the current runtime workspace and shows a lightweight detail drill-down without mutating them.
texts.admin_sessions_empty: No saved sessions were found in the current workspace.
texts.admin_skills_intro: This page reads the current workspace skill root at {path} and merges it with bundled built-in skills using the same loader the runtime uses.
texts.admin_skills_empty: No skills are available in the current runtime workspace.
texts.admin_cron_intro: This page reads the workspace cron store at {path} and shows the persisted scheduler state. It is read-only; use chat commands or file editing when you need to change jobs.
texts.admin_cron_empty: No cron jobs are currently saved in this workspace.
texts.admin_config_intro: This page saves the active instance config file at {config_path}. Runtime hot-reload still follows hahobot's current rules; provider credentials, connection settings, and some routing changes may still require a gateway restart.
texts.admin_config_saved_reloaded: Config saved, and the current instance has force-reloaded the runtime settings that support hot reload.
texts.admin_weixin_intro: Use this page to request a fresh QR code for the built-in personal Weixin channel. Successful QR login saves the token into the channel state file; if channels.weixin.token is also set in config.json, that configured token still takes precedence at runtime.
texts.admin_weixin_config_token_notice: A non-empty channels.weixin.token is configured in config.json. The runtime currently prefers that configured token over the saved QR-login state file, so clear or update the config token if you want QR login state to take effect after restart.
texts.admin_weixin_state_file_notice: The QR login flow saves the token into the Weixin state file. Restart the current process when you need the channel to reconnect with the freshly saved login state.
texts.admin_weixin_qr_desc: Keep this page open while scanning. The admin UI polls the Weixin login status every few seconds and refreshes the QR code automatically when the upstream marks it expired.
texts.admin_weixin_qr_no_image: QR image rendering is unavailable. Install the optional Weixin dependencies if you want the admin page to draw the QR code directly; the raw scan URL is still shown below.
texts.admin_weixin_scan_hint: Scan the QR code in Weixin. If the upstream redirects polling to another host after the first scan, this page follows that redirect automatically.
texts.admin_weixin_saved_state_desc: This mirrors the local state used by the built-in Weixin channel. It is separate from the visual channels.weixin.token field in config.json.
texts.admin_weixin_missing_session: The requested Weixin QR session was not found or has expired.
texts.admin_weixin_confirmed_flash: Weixin login confirmed and the token was saved to the local state file.
texts.admin_weixin_error_missing_token: Login was confirmed, but the upstream response did not include a bot token.
texts.admin_weixin_error_expired_too_many: The QR code expired too many times. Start a fresh login session and try again.
texts.admin_weixin_start_failed: Failed to start Weixin QR login: {error}
texts.admin_config_advanced_desc: Use the raw config.json editor when you need provider credentials, multi-instance channel blocks, or fields not exposed in the visual form.
texts.admin_config_reload_notice: After saving, this instance immediately force-reloads configuration fields that support hot reload. Each visual field below is tagged as “Hot reload” or “Requires restart”.
texts.admin_config_jump_desc: Jump to a config section, edit the fields there, then save the full form once.
texts.admin_commands_intro: This page lists the chat slash commands currently registered in AgentLoop. Usage examples show the primary command forms, and aliases are listed separately when a command exposes them.
texts.admin_commands_list_desc: Select a command on the left to view its details, usage, and aliases on the right.
texts.admin_commands_note_lang: /language is an alias for /lang. The selected language is stored per session.
texts.admin_commands_note_persona: /persona set <name> tries to archive current-session memory first, then switches persona and starts a new session.
texts.admin_commands_note_skill: /skill search uses direct HTTP search. /skill derive creates a local workspace draft and supports --force for explicit overwrite. /skill install, /skill list, and /skill update still require local Node.js and npx.
texts.admin_commands_note_mcp: Bare /mcp behaves the same as /mcp list.
texts.admin_commands_note_session: In gateway chats, /session use and /session new reroute only the current chat. Use /session use default to return to the origin session key.
texts.admin_commands_note_stop: /stop cancels active tasks and subagents for the current session.
texts.admin_commands_note_restart: /restart restarts the current bot process in place, and channel connections reconnect after the process comes back.
texts.admin_commands_note_update: /update supports check, force, and bridge: the default mode runs git pull --ff-only, uv sync --locked --all-extras, and refreshes the local WhatsApp bridge when enabled; check is dry-run only; force skips the clean-working-tree precheck; bridge only refreshes the WhatsApp bridge and then restarts.
texts.admin_commands_note_compact: /compact reuses the existing automatic token-consolidation path; it does not create a separate memory pipeline.
texts.admin_config_section_agents_desc: These fields control the default model, provider routing, context window, and runtime behavior for this instance.
texts.admin_config_section_providers_desc: Common providers.* blocks for this instance. These values are restart-required routing credentials; less common providers still belong in raw JSON.
texts.admin_provider_group_openrouter_desc: Gateway-first setup for broad model coverage through one OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
texts.admin_provider_group_openai_desc: Direct GPT access without an extra gateway layer.
texts.admin_provider_group_anthropic_desc: Native Claude credentials for direct Anthropic routing.
texts.admin_provider_group_deepseek_desc: Direct DeepSeek credentials for native DeepSeek access.
texts.admin_provider_group_custom_desc: Any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint, including local servers and private gateways.
texts.admin_provider_group_ollama_desc: Local Ollama base URL for model auto-detection and direct routing.
texts.admin_provider_group_vllm_desc: Local vLLM or another self-hosted OpenAI-compatible server.
texts.admin_config_section_gateway_desc: Host, port, admin, and dashboard-status settings here only affect the instance started from this config file.
texts.admin_config_section_status_push_desc: Optionally push runtime status directly to a Star-Office-UI instance over HTTP. Choose guest mode for join-agent / agent-push, or main mode to drive the built-in main agent via /set_state.
texts.admin_config_section_channels_desc: Focused visual cards for common single-instance channel credentials. When a channel uses multi-instance config, the visual editor switches to a read-only notice and leaves the full block in raw JSON.
texts.admin_channel_group_whatsapp_desc: Single bridge URL and token for the built-in local WhatsApp bridge integration.
texts.admin_channel_group_telegram_desc: Single bot token and optional proxy for direct Telegram polling.
texts.admin_channel_group_discord_desc: Single bot gateway settings and token for the direct Discord connection.
texts.admin_channel_group_feishu_desc: Single app credentials for the long-connection Feishu bot.
texts.admin_channel_group_dingtalk_desc: Single Stream mode app credentials for DingTalk.
texts.admin_channel_group_slack_desc: Single Socket Mode credentials for the Slack bot.
texts.admin_channel_group_qq_desc: Single QQ bot credentials for the botpy-based channel.
texts.admin_channel_group_matrix_desc: Single Matrix account credentials and homeserver endpoint.
texts.admin_channel_group_weixin_desc: QR-login personal Weixin channel settings for the built-in HTTP long-poll integration. Configure the allowlist plus optional saved token and local state path here.
texts.admin_channel_group_multi_instance_notic: This channel currently uses multi-instance config under {path}. Keep editing it in raw JSON so the existing instance blocks stay untouched.
texts.admin_config_section_web_desc: Configure the web search provider, proxy, and whether tools are restricted to the workspace.
texts.admin_config_section_exec_desc: Control whether the built-in exec tool is available, how long commands may run, and which extra PATH entries are appended.
texts.admin_config_section_image_desc: Configure the built-in image_gen tool, including model, proxy, timeout, and default reference image.
texts.admin_config_section_mem0_desc: This section controls the active memory.user backend and the memory.user.mem0 runtime config. Set memory.user.backend=mem0 to read/write user memory through Mem0, or keep file as primary and enable shadowWriteMem0 for parallel writes. The visual form covers the common Mem0 provider fields; keep uncommon provider extras in raw JSON.
texts.admin_config_section_memorix_desc: Dedicated visual fields for the built-in memorix workspace-memory integration. This only edits tools.mcpServers.memorix; other MCP servers still belong in raw JSON.
texts.admin_config_section_channel_runtime_des: These are generic delivery/runtime switches that are usually safe to hot-reload.
texts.admin_config_section_voice_desc: Centralized channels.voiceReply settings for OpenAI, Edge TTS, and GPT-SoVITS.
texts.admin_config_section_edge_desc: Voice and pacing settings used when provider=edge.
texts.admin_config_section_sovits_desc: Local SoVITS endpoint and reference-audio settings used when provider=sovits.
texts.admin_config_agents_workspace_tooltip: Default workspace path. Leave it blank to inherit <config-dir>/workspace. Persona files, memory, skills, and workspace/out artifacts all live under this root; restarting the current gateway process is still required before the runtime workspace actually switches.
texts.admin_config_agents_model_tooltip: Default model identifier for this instance. New sessions use this value unless a persona, channel, or CLI override replaces it; it also affects provider auto-detection and feature availability.
texts.admin_config_agents_provider_hint: Use auto to match from the model name. Provider credentials are still better managed in raw JSON.
texts.admin_config_agents_provider_tooltip: Force a specific provider registry name, or keep auto. When this is not auto, model routing follows the selected provider instead of mainly relying on model-prefix detection.
texts.admin_config_agents_provider_pool_strate: Leave targets empty to disable the pool and fall back to agents.defaults.provider.

Optional multi-provider routing policy. failover tries targets in order until one succeeds; round_robin rotates the starting target on each request and still falls through on provider errors. Changing this routing requires a restart.
texts.admin_config_agents_provider_pool_target: Edit provider targets row by row. Leave every row blank to remove providerPool when you save.

Ordered provider targets for the pool. Each row must choose a provider, and may optionally include a per-target model override. When a target omits model, hahobot falls back to agents.defaults.model. Changing pool targets requires a restart.
texts.admin_config_agents_max_tokens_tooltip: Upper bound for one model response. Higher values allow longer replies, but also increase latency, cost, and the chance of hitting provider-side output limits.
texts.admin_config_agents_context_window_toolt: Maximum context budget available to each model call. Larger values keep more history, tool output, and system context alive, but use more tokens and still cannot exceed the model's real context limit.
texts.admin_config_agents_temperature_tooltip: Sampling randomness. Lower values are more stable and conservative; higher values are more varied and less predictable. Tool-heavy or companion flows usually prefer lower settings.
texts.admin_config_agents_max_tool_iterations_: Maximum number of tool-call rounds allowed in a single task. This prevents runaway loops; setting it too low can make complex workflows stop before they finish.
texts.admin_config_agents_reasoning_effort_too: Thinking intensity for models that support reasoning effort. Leave it blank to follow the model or provider default; higher levels usually cost more latency and tokens but may improve complex-task stability.
texts.admin_config_agents_timezone_tooltip: Default timezone injected into runtime prompts, scheduled jobs, and time parsing. Use an IANA timezone name such as Asia/Shanghai or America/Los_Angeles.
texts.admin_config_providers_openrouter_api_ke: API key for OpenRouter. This is the common gateway choice when you want broad multi-model coverage behind one endpoint.
texts.admin_config_providers_openrouter_api_ba: Optional API base override for OpenRouter-compatible routing. Leave it at the usual default unless you are intentionally using a different gateway URL.
texts.admin_config_providers_openai_api_key_to: API key for direct OpenAI access. Use this when you want GPT models without going through a separate gateway.
texts.admin_config_providers_openai_api_base_t: Optional base URL override for OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Leave it blank for the official OpenAI base, or set it when a compatible proxy/gateway must be used.
texts.admin_config_providers_anthropic_api_key: API key for direct Anthropic access. This is used for native Claude routing rather than OpenAI-compatible gateways.
texts.admin_config_providers_anthropic_api_bas: Optional Anthropic base URL override. Most setups should leave this blank unless a compatible enterprise proxy is required.
texts.admin_config_providers_deepseek_api_key_: API key for direct DeepSeek access. Useful when you want DeepSeek models without routing through another gateway.
texts.admin_config_providers_deepseek_api_base: Optional DeepSeek base URL override. Leave blank for the usual direct endpoint unless a compatible proxy is involved.
texts.admin_config_providers_custom_api_key_to: API key for any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint. If your local server does not require auth, a non-empty placeholder string is usually enough.
texts.admin_config_providers_custom_api_base_t: Base URL for a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint such as LM Studio, llama.cpp, Fireworks, or an internal gateway.
texts.admin_config_providers_custom_extra_head: Extra HTTP headers for the custom endpoint, entered as a JSON object. Use this for gateway-specific auth or routing headers such as APP-Code.
texts.admin_config_providers_ollama_api_base_t: Base URL for Ollama's OpenAI-compatible endpoint. This is what lets plain local models such as llama3.2 resolve through Ollama.
texts.admin_config_providers_vllm_api_base_too: Base URL for a local vLLM or another self-hosted OpenAI-compatible server. Use this when local models are served outside Ollama.
texts.admin_config_gateway_host_tooltip: Bind address for this gateway process. It controls where the built-in HTTP server listens for /healthz and /admin; local-only setups often use 127.0.0.1, while externally exposed services usually use 0.0.0.0.
texts.admin_config_gateway_port_tooltip: Listen port for this gateway HTTP server. A restart is usually required before a new port takes effect.
texts.admin_config_gateway_heartbeat_enabled_t: Enable periodic heartbeat runs. When on, hahobot can inject recurring runtime summaries or keep-alive context, which is useful for persistent companion or status-tracking flows.
texts.admin_config_gateway_heartbeat_interval_: Heartbeat interval in seconds. This only matters when gateway.heartbeat.enabled is turned on.
texts.admin_config_gateway_heartbeat_keep_rece: How many recent messages should stay alongside heartbeat context. Higher values keep more short-term detail but use more context budget.
texts.admin_config_gateway_cron_max_sleep_tool: Maximum sleep interval for the workspace cron scheduler, in milliseconds. Even when the next known job is far away, hahobot wakes at least this often to reload cron/jobs.json and discover externally added or edited jobs.
texts.admin_config_gateway_admin_enabled_toolt: Expose the built-in /admin page for this instance. When it is off, the route returns 404; when it is on, a non-empty gateway.admin.authKey is required.
texts.admin_config_gateway_admin_auth_key_tool: Shared secret required to log into the admin page for this instance. Use a long random string; it must be non-empty whenever gateway.admin.enabled=true.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_enabled_tool: Expose the lightweight /status dashboard endpoint for this instance. When disabled, the route returns 404; when enabled, it returns the current Star Office style runtime status payload.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_auth_key_hin: Leave blank to allow local unauthenticated status reads.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_auth_key_too: Optional bearer token required by GET /status. When this is non-empty, callers must send Authorization: Bearer <authKey>; unlike admin login, it may be left blank for trusted local dashboards.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_push_enabled: Push the latest hahobot runtime status directly to a Star-Office-UI instance through its HTTP API. This is independent from exposing the local /status endpoint.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_push_mode_hi: Use guest for invited-agent mode, or main to drive the built-in main office agent via /set_state.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_push_mode_to: Select how hahobot should appear in Star-Office-UI. guest uses /join-agent plus /agent-push and requires a joinKey. main updates the built-in main agent through /set_state and does not require a joinKey.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_push_office_: Use the Star-Office-UI base URL, for example https://office.example.com.
Base URL of the Star-Office-UI instance. In guest mode hahobot appends /join-agent and /agent-push; in main mode it pushes to /set_state.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_push_join_ke: Join key issued by the Star-Office-UI room. It is required only in guest mode, where hahobot registers as an invited agent and reuses the key for later updates.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_push_agent_n: Display name shown for this hahobot instance in Star-Office-UI guest mode. Leave it at hahobot unless you need multiple invited bots with different names.
texts.admin_config_gateway_status_push_timeout: HTTP timeout in seconds for join and push calls to Star-Office-UI. Increase it if the office instance is behind a slow proxy or remote tunnel.
texts.admin_config_channels_whatsapp_enabled_t: Enable the single-instance WhatsApp bridge integration. This visual card only writes the flat single-instance block; if you need multiple bridges, switch to raw JSON.
texts.admin_config_channels_whatsapp_bridge_ur: WebSocket URL of the local WhatsApp bridge, usually ws://localhost:3001. This is the bridge process started by the WhatsApp login flow.
texts.admin_config_channels_whatsapp_bridge_to: Optional shared token used to authenticate against the local WhatsApp bridge. Leave it blank only if the bridge intentionally runs without token protection.
texts.admin_config_channels_telegram_enabled_t: Enable the single Telegram bot config. Multi-bot instances setups stay in raw JSON.
texts.admin_config_channels_telegram_token_too: Bot token issued by @BotFather for the Telegram bot.
texts.admin_config_channels_telegram_proxy_too: Optional HTTP/SOCKS proxy for Telegram API traffic, for example socks5://127.0.0.1:1080. Leave blank for direct connections.
texts.admin_config_channels_telegram_stream_ed: Minimum delay in seconds between streamed edit_message_text calls. Lower values feel more live but can hit Telegram rate limits sooner; higher values reduce edit pressure.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_enabled_to: Enable the single Discord bot config. Use raw JSON when you need multiple Discord instances.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_token_tool: Bot token from the Discord Developer Portal.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_gateway_ur: Discord gateway URL. Most setups should keep the standard wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_intents_to: Gateway intents bitmask used by the Discord bot. Leave the default unless you intentionally change subscribed event scopes in the Discord app.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_proxy_tool: Optional HTTP/SOCKS proxy for the Discord connection. Leave it blank for direct connections; if you add proxy auth, set both username and password together.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_proxy_user: Optional proxy username for Discord. If you set this, also set channels.discord.proxyPassword; partial proxy credentials are ignored at runtime.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_proxy_pass: Optional proxy password for Discord. If you set this, also set channels.discord.proxyUsername; partial proxy credentials are ignored at runtime.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_streaming_: Enable progressive Discord reply edits before the final response is complete. Turning it off falls back to non-streamed final sends.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_read_recei: Reaction emoji added when hahobot has seen the Discord message and started handling it.
texts.admin_config_channels_discord_working_em: Reaction emoji added while hahobot is still working on the Discord reply after the delay threshold is reached.
Delay in seconds before the Discord working emoji is added. Raise it if short replies should stay quiet; lower it if you want faster visible feedback.
texts.admin_config_channels_feishu_enabled_too: Enable the single Feishu/Lark bot config for long-connection mode.
texts.admin_config_channels_feishu_app_id_tool: App ID from the Feishu/Lark Open Platform.
texts.admin_config_channels_feishu_app_secret_: App secret from the Feishu/Lark Open Platform.
texts.admin_config_channels_feishu_encrypt_key: Optional encrypt key for event subscription payloads.
texts.admin_config_channels_feishu_verificatio: Optional verification token used when Feishu validates event subscriptions.
texts.admin_config_channels_dingtalk_enabled_t: Enable the single DingTalk Stream mode bot config.
texts.admin_config_channels_dingtalk_client_id: DingTalk AppKey / client ID for Stream mode.
texts.admin_config_channels_dingtalk_client_se: DingTalk AppSecret / client secret for Stream mode.
texts.admin_config_channels_slack_enabled_tool: Enable the single Slack bot config for Socket Mode.
texts.admin_config_channels_slack_bot_token_to: Slack bot token, usually starting with xoxb-.
texts.admin_config_channels_slack_app_token_to: Slack app token for Socket Mode, usually starting with xapp-.
texts.admin_config_channels_qq_enabled_tooltip: Enable the single QQ bot config.
texts.admin_config_channels_matrix_enabled_too: Enable the single Matrix account config.
texts.admin_config_channels_matrix_homeserver_: Base URL of the Matrix homeserver, such as https://matrix.org or your self-hosted server.
texts.admin_config_channels_matrix_access_toke: Access token for the Matrix bot/account.
texts.admin_config_channels_matrix_user_id_too: Full Matrix user ID, for example @bot:matrix.org.
texts.admin_config_channels_matrix_device_id_t: Optional Matrix device ID used for login state and encryption storage.
texts.admin_config_channels_weixin_enabled_too: Enable the single personal Weixin config. This card targets the built-in QR-login long-poll channel and writes only the flat single-instance block.
texts.admin_config_channels_weixin_allow_from_: Allowed Weixin sender IDs. Separate multiple IDs with commas. Use * to allow all users, or leave it empty to deny all.
Comma-separated sender IDs. Example: wxid_xxx, wxid_yyy or *.
texts.admin_config_channels_weixin_token_toolt: Optional saved bot token for the ilinkai Weixin API. You can leave this blank and run hahobot channels login weixin to log in by QR code; hahobot will persist the token afterward.
texts.admin_config_channels_weixin_route_tag_t: Optional SKRouteTag header value required by some upstream Weixin deployments for request routing.
texts.admin_config_channels_weixin_state_dir_t: Optional local directory used to persist the Weixin token and sync buffer. Leave blank to use hahobot's default runtime path.
texts.admin_config_channels_weixin_poll_timeou: Long-poll timeout in seconds for getupdates. Most setups can keep the default unless the upstream requires a different value.
texts.admin_config_channels_wecom_enabled_tool: Enable the single WeCom AI Bot config.
texts.admin_config_tools_restrict_to_workspace: Restrict built-in file and shell tools to the active workspace. This reduces the risk of out-of-scope access and is recommended for production or long-running local deployments.
texts.admin_config_web_proxy_tooltip: HTTP/SOCKS proxy used by built-in web tools, for example http://127.0.0.1:7890 or socks5://127.0.0.1:1080. Leave it blank for direct connections.
texts.admin_config_web_search_provider_tooltip: Backend used by web_search. brave requires an official API key, searxng is suitable for self-hosted or internal search setups, and duckduckgo works without extra credentials.
texts.admin_config_web_search_api_key_tooltip: Brave Search API key. It is only used when the search provider is brave; it is ignored for searxng and duckduckgo.
texts.admin_config_web_search_base_url_hint: Required only when the provider is searxng.
texts.admin_config_web_search_base_url_tooltip: Base URL of the SearXNG instance, such as http://localhost:8080. It is only required when the provider is searxng; brave and duckduckgo ignore this field.
texts.admin_config_web_search_max_results_tool: Maximum number of search results returned to the model per call. Larger values improve coverage but also add more context cost and noise.
texts.admin_config_exec_enable_tooltip: Register the built-in shell exec tool for this runtime. Turning it off removes shell-command execution entirely until the setting is turned back on.
texts.admin_config_exec_timeout_tooltip: Per-command timeout in seconds for the built-in exec tool. Increase it for slower local tooling, or lower it to fail fast on runaway commands.
texts.admin_config_exec_path_append_hint: Append extra directories using your platform PATH separator, for example /usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin.
texts.admin_config_exec_path_append_tooltip: Extra PATH entries appended before shell commands run. Use this when tools such as ffmpeg, git, or firewall helpers live outside the default PATH inherited by the gateway process.
texts.admin_config_exec_allowed_env_keys_hint: Separate keys with commas or new lines, for example JAVA_HOME or GOPATH.
texts.admin_config_exec_allowed_env_keys_toolt: Allowlist of parent environment variable names that may be forwarded into shell subprocesses. Keep this list narrow so the runtime does not leak the whole gateway environment to the exec tool.
texts.admin_config_exec_sandbox_hint: Leave blank to disable sandboxing; set bwrap to use Bubblewrap when it is installed.
texts.admin_config_exec_sandbox_tooltip: Optional shell-tool sandbox backend. bwrap runs commands through Bubblewrap and may require extra host setup; leaving it blank keeps the current unsandboxed exec behavior.
texts.admin_config_image_enabled_tooltip: Register the built-in image_gen tool. When enabled, generated images are written under <workspace>/out/image_gen and can be attached to later user delivery steps.
texts.admin_config_image_api_key_tooltip: API key used by the image generation service. This is commonly separate from the chat-model provider when image generation is routed through a different service.
texts.admin_config_image_base_url_tooltip: Base API URL for image generation requests. Use it to point image generation at an OpenAI-compatible gateway, local service, or third-party image endpoint.
texts.admin_config_image_model_tooltip: Default image generation model name. Style, quality, and reference-image support depend on the image backend you connect here.
texts.admin_config_image_proxy_tooltip: Dedicated HTTP/SOCKS proxy for image generation requests. Leave it blank to avoid overriding the direct connection behavior.
texts.admin_config_image_timeout_tooltip: Request timeout in seconds for image generation. Slow image models often need a higher value than text requests.
texts.admin_config_image_reference_image_toolt: Default reference-image value. If a model call does not provide an explicit reference image, this value can be used instead; special placeholders such as __default__ can resolve to persona-manifest reference images.
texts.admin_config_mem0_backend_tooltip: Select the active user-memory backend. file keeps using persona MEMORY.md for prompt injection, while mem0 resolves context and writes turns through Mem0 at runtime.
texts.admin_config_mem0_shadow_write_tooltip: When memory.user.backend=file, also write completed turns to Mem0 in parallel. This lets you keep file-backed prompt memory while warming or mirroring a Mem0 store.
texts.admin_config_mem0_mode_tooltip: Mem0 runtime mode. hahobot currently supports the embedded OSS mode exposed by the Python Mem0 SDK.
texts.admin_config_mem0_llm_provider_tooltip: Provider name used by Mem0's LLM side, for example openai, ollama, or another future hosted provider identifier.
texts.admin_config_mem0_llm_api_key_tooltip: API key for the Mem0 LLM provider. This is useful for paid/cloud-hosted models; local services may leave it blank or use a dummy value if the gateway expects one.
texts.admin_config_mem0_llm_url_tooltip: Request or gateway URL for the Mem0 LLM provider. Point it at a hosted OpenAI-compatible API, a private gateway, or a local service.
texts.admin_config_mem0_llm_model_tooltip: Model or deployment name used by Mem0's LLM provider. A future Mem0 integration should prefer this explicit model field.
texts.admin_config_mem0_llm_headers_tooltip: Extra request headers for the Mem0 LLM provider, entered as a JSON object. Use this for custom auth headers, organization IDs, or gateway-specific header requirements.
texts.admin_config_mem0_llm_config_tooltip: Extended provider-specific config for the Mem0 LLM provider, entered as JSON. Use it for options not covered by the visual form.
texts.admin_config_mem0_embedder_provider_tool: Embedding provider name used by Mem0, such as openai, ollama, or another compatible embedding service.
texts.admin_config_mem0_embedder_api_key_toolt: API key for the Mem0 embedding provider. Paid hosted embedding services usually need a separate key here.
texts.admin_config_mem0_embedder_url_tooltip: URL used by the Mem0 embedding provider. It can point to a hosted embedding API, a local gateway, or a self-hosted compatible service.
texts.admin_config_mem0_embedder_model_tooltip: Embedding model name used by Mem0, such as text-embedding-3-small or a local embedding model identifier.
texts.admin_config_mem0_embedder_headers_toolt: Extra request headers for the Mem0 embedding provider, entered as a JSON object. This is useful when an embedding gateway expects custom headers.
texts.admin_config_mem0_embedder_config_toolti: Extended provider-specific config for the Mem0 embedding provider, entered as JSON. Keep provider-only extras here.
texts.admin_config_mem0_vector_store_provider_: Vector-store provider name for Mem0, for example qdrant or another hosted vector database. This visual form only covers the common provider-level fields.
texts.admin_config_mem0_vector_store_api_key_t: API key for the vector database. Hosted vector stores often require their own credential, while local deployments may not.
texts.admin_config_mem0_vector_store_url_toolt: URL for the vector database service, such as a hosted Qdrant/Pinecone endpoint or a locally exposed instance.
texts.admin_config_mem0_vector_store_model_too: Explicit model field kept for compatibility with the unified provider shape. Many vector stores will ignore it, but future hosted providers may use it.
texts.admin_config_mem0_vector_store_headers_t: Extra headers for vector-store requests, entered as a JSON object. Use this for hosted Qdrant, Pinecone, or gateway-specific auth headers.
texts.admin_config_mem0_vector_store_config_to: Extended vector-store config entered as JSON. Put collection names, namespaces, or other provider-specific options here.
texts.admin_config_mem0_metadata_tooltip: Top-level Mem0 metadata object, entered as JSON. These fields are attached to Mem0 writes so you can tag memories with tenant, environment, or other routing metadata.
texts.admin_config_memorix_enabled_tooltip: Control whether the built-in Memorix visual settings are written into tools.mcpServers.memorix for this instance. When enabled, hahobot auto-loads the Memorix skill and calls memorix_session_start on first use in each chat session.
texts.admin_config_memorix_type_tooltip: Memorix MCP transport type. Leave it blank for auto-detection: stdio when command is set, or HTTP/SSE when url is set. Local setups usually use stdio or streamableHttp.
texts.admin_config_memorix_command_hint: For local stdio, this is usually memorix.
texts.admin_config_memorix_command_tooltip: Command used to start Memorix in stdio mode. If the Memorix binary is already on PATH, memorix is the common value.
texts.admin_config_memorix_args_hint: Separate arguments with commas or new lines, for example serve.
texts.admin_config_memorix_args_tooltip: Arguments passed to the stdio command. Commas and new lines are both accepted; the common local startup is memorix serve.
texts.admin_config_memorix_url_tooltip: MCP URL used when Memorix runs as an HTTP service. URLs ending in /sse are treated as SSE; otherwise streamableHttp is the usual choice.
texts.admin_config_memorix_tool_timeout_toolti: Per-call timeout in seconds for Memorix MCP tools. Increase it if indexing, searching, or writes are slow.
texts.admin_config_channel_send_progress_toolt: Send intermediate progress lines back to users while the task is running. Turn it off if you want quieter channels that only receive final results.
texts.admin_config_channel_send_tool_hints_too: Expose tool-call hints such as file reads, shell commands, or image-generation actions to users while a task is executing. Useful for debugging; often too noisy for normal companion flows.
texts.admin_config_channel_send_max_retries_to: Maximum number of outbound delivery attempts, including the first send. Higher values improve resilience during short network failures but also keep a failing send path alive longer.
texts.admin_config_channel_transcription_provi: Speech-to-text backend used when channels need to transcribe inbound audio. groq is the default fast path; openai is useful when you want transcription to stay on the same provider stack.
texts.admin_config_channel_transcription_langu: Optional ISO-639 language hint such as en, zh, or ja.
Optional language hint passed to the configured transcription backend. Leave blank for auto-detection; changes are hot-reloaded into running channel instances.
texts.admin_config_voice_enabled_tooltip: Enable voice attachments on supported outbound channels. When enabled, hahobot usually keeps the normal text reply and adds a synthesized audio reply beside it.
texts.admin_config_voice_channels_hint: Separate channel names with commas or newlines, for example telegram or qq.
texts.admin_config_voice_channels_tooltip: List of channels that should receive voice attachments. This only controls where TTS is attempted; it does not disable the normal text reply.
texts.admin_config_voice_provider_tooltip: TTS backend used for voice replies: openai, edge, or sovits. Each provider expects a different set of parameters and output capabilities.
texts.admin_config_voice_model_tooltip: Model or deployment name used by OpenAI-compatible TTS. It is usually not the primary parameter when the provider is edge or sovits.
texts.admin_config_voice_voice_tooltip: Voice preset name for OpenAI-compatible TTS, such as alloy. This field is usually ignored unless the provider is openai.
texts.admin_config_voice_instructions_tooltip: Extra style guidance passed into TTS synthesis, such as softer tone, slower delivery, or more restrained emotion. Persona-local VOICE.json can still refine or override this.
texts.admin_config_voice_speed_tooltip: Speaking-speed multiplier for providers that support it. Leave it blank to use the provider default; extreme values can make the audio sound unnatural.
texts.admin_config_voice_response_format_toolt: Audio format returned by the TTS endpoint. QQ local voice delivery usually needs silk to send the audio directly.
texts.admin_config_voice_api_key_tooltip: API key for the dedicated voice-reply backend. This is often needed when TTS is served by a different provider than the current chat model.
texts.admin_config_voice_api_base_hint: Leave blank to reuse the active conversation provider URL. Raw JSON still accepts url.
texts.admin_config_voice_api_base_tooltip: Override the TTS endpoint independently from the current chat provider. This is useful when chat and speech are routed through different gateways or local services.
texts.admin_config_edge_voice_tooltip: Microsoft Edge TTS voice name, for example zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural. It only matters when the provider is edge.
texts.admin_config_edge_rate_tooltip: Edge TTS rate adjustment, such as +0% or -10%. Use it to fine-tune speaking pace.
texts.admin_config_edge_volume_tooltip: Edge TTS volume adjustment, such as +0% or +20%. Use it when the generated voice is too quiet or too loud.
texts.admin_config_sovits_api_url_tooltip: HTTP endpoint of your local or remote GPT-SoVITS service, such as http://127.0.0.1:9880. This is where voice-reply requests go when the provider is sovits.
texts.admin_config_sovits_refer_wav_path_toolt: Reference audio file used for custom voice cloning. It is typically paired with the matching transcript to reproduce the target timbre more reliably.
texts.admin_config_sovits_prompt_text_tooltip: Transcript corresponding to the reference audio. GPT-SoVITS uses it to align the reference voice with pronunciation, cadence, and speaking style.
texts.admin_config_sovits_prompt_language_tool: Language code of the reference transcript, such as zh or en. It should match the real content of the reference audio.
texts.admin_config_sovits_text_language_toolti: Language code of the target text that will be synthesized. A mismatch here can cause segmentation, pause, or pronunciation problems.
texts.admin_config_sovits_cut_punc_tooltip: Punctuation set used to split text before GPT-SoVITS synthesis. Good segmentation often improves pause placement and generation stability on long sentences.
texts.admin_config_sovits_top_k_tooltip: Top-k sampling parameter for GPT-SoVITS. Lower values are usually more stable; higher values can sound richer but less predictable.
texts.admin_config_sovits_top_p_tooltip: Top-p sampling parameter for GPT-SoVITS. Together with top-k and temperature, it shapes how stable or varied the generated voice becomes.
texts.admin_config_sovits_temperature_tooltip: Sampling temperature for GPT-SoVITS. Lower values usually stay closer to the reference voice, while higher values produce more variation at the cost of stability.
texts.admin_error_admin_auth_required: When gateway.admin.enabled is true, gateway.admin.authKey must be a non-empty string.
texts.admin_error_runtime_reload_failed: The config file was saved, but runtime reload failed: {error}
texts.admin_error_provider_pool_target_provide: Each providerPool target row must choose a provider before saving.
texts.admin_card_create_persona_desc: Use a short directory-safe name. The editor will create SOUL / USER / memory scaffolding automatically.
texts.admin_error_invalid_persona_name: Persona names must be 1-64 characters, use letters, digits, _ or -, and cannot be default.
texts.admin_persona_intro: Edit the prompt and metadata files for this persona in the active runtime workspace. In most cases SOUL.md and USER.md should stay present; PROFILE.md is the optional user-profile layer, INSIGHTS.md stores learned collaboration guidance, and the rest are optional overlays.
texts.admin_persona_soul_desc: Core persona identity, values, and long-lived behavioral baseline. This is where you define who the persona is.
texts.admin_persona_user_desc: Default stance toward the user, relationship framing, and interaction boundaries. This is where you define how the persona relates to you.
texts.admin_persona_profile_desc: Optional long-term user profile: stable preferences, habits, facts, and recurring collaboration patterns. Use this for the user model, not the persona's own identity.
texts.admin_persona_insights_desc: Optional learned collaboration insights: effective workflows, decision heuristics, recurring pitfalls, and strategy notes discovered over time. Use this for reflective guidance, not raw user facts.
texts.admin_persona_style_desc: Optional response-style overlay, such as favorite phrasing, reply length, formatting habits, or speaking mannerisms.
texts.admin_persona_lore_desc: Optional worldbuilding, backstory, and supporting setting detail. Use it for extra context instead of repeating the core persona.
texts.admin_persona_voice_desc: Optional voice/TTS override settings. Use this to pin a persona-specific provider, voice preset, or SoVITS reference parameters.
texts.admin_persona_scene_desc: Edit the common `/scene` shortcuts here without hand-writing the whole st_manifest.json. Unknown manifest fields still stay available in the raw JSON editor below.
texts.admin_persona_scene_reference_hint: Optional fallback reference image path used by `/scene` when a scene-specific image is not configured.
texts.admin_persona_scene_references_hint: One mapping per line using scene = path. Example: comfort = assets/comfort.png.
texts.admin_persona_scene_prompts_hint: One mapping per line using scene = prompt. These lines are appended to the built-in prompt for that scene.
texts.admin_persona_scene_captions_hint: One mapping per line using scene = caption. Captions may use {{persona}} as a placeholder.
texts.admin_persona_scene_tags_hint: Comma-separated tags removed only from final user-visible replies, for example inner, thought.
texts.admin_persona_scene_map_invalid: Scene mapping lines must use name = value. Invalid line: {line}
texts.admin_persona_scene_preview_desc: Generate a one-off `/scene` preview for this persona using the current runtime image settings.
texts.admin_persona_scene_template_invalid_nam: Scene names must start with a letter or digit, use only letters, digits, _ or -, and stay within 64 characters.
texts.admin_persona_scene_template_save_failed: Failed to save the scene template: {error}
texts.admin_persona_manifest_desc: Optional persona metadata such as response_filter_tags, default or scene-specific reference images, scene_prompts / scene_captions, or imported companion metadata.
texts.admin_persona_optional_hint: Leave PROFILE.md, INSIGHTS.md, STYLE.md, LORE.md, VOICE.json, or st_manifest.json blank to remove that file.
texts.admin_persona_memory_metadata_desc: Track how much structured memory metadata is present in PROFILE.md and INSIGHTS.md. Structured comments use the form <!-- hahobot-meta: confidence=high last_verified=YYYY-MM-DD -->.
texts.admin_persona_memory_metadata_empty: No structured metadata or legacy (verify) markers are currently detected in this file.
texts.admin_persona_migrate_desc: If this persona still uses an old profile-style USER.md, migrate obvious user-profile sections into PROFILE.md and workflow guidance into INSIGHTS.md. Existing target content is preserved and the remaining USER.md stays focused on relationship framing.
texts.admin_persona_migration_preview_desc: Review the exact post-migration USER.md, PROFILE.md, and INSIGHTS.md content before running the migration.
texts.admin_persona_migration_none_desc: No obvious legacy profile-style USER.md sections are currently detected for migration.
texts.admin_persona_migrated: Legacy USER.md migration complete: moved {profile} section(s) into PROFILE.md and {insights} section(s) into INSIGHTS.md.
texts.admin_json_object_required: JSON content must be an object.
texts.admin_json_array_required: JSON content must be an array.

telegram_commands.stchar: List, inspect, or load persona assets
